title: COUNTIF vs COUNTIFS: one letter, one big difference
meta_desc: COUNTIF vs COUNTIFS: multiple criteria, argument patterns, OR-counting, and verified compatibility across Excel, Google Sheets, and LibreOffice.
intro: COUNTIFS is COUNTIF's multi-condition sibling — and unlike the SUMIF/SUMIFS pair, the argument order doesn't flip, so the safest habit is simply: always use COUNTIFS.

cols: Same — COUNTIFS is AND-only; use SUMPRODUCT for true OR
Universal (Excel 2007+, every Sheets & LibreOffice version we test)

when.use_when: Legacy sheets that already use it — there's no functional advantage over a one-pair COUNTIFS.
Everything new. Same syntax for one condition or five, no relearning when requirements grow.

compat_note: Both execute correctly in every version of all three apps we test. One shared quirk to know: criteria ranges must be the same size, and text criteria over ~255 characters fail in Excel — neither limit differs between the two functions.
